I’ve been working with this law firm for the past few years, and I truly can’t recommend them enough. As a business owner based in San Diego, having a reliable, knowledgeable, and responsive legal team is absolutely essential and they have consistently exceeded expectations.

Hollie, in particular, has been incredible to work with. She is always professional, extremely responsive, and thorough in everything she does. Whether we need help reviewing contracts, navigating legal questions, or ensuring we’re compliant with California regulations, she handles it quickly and with great attention to detail. It gives me so much peace of mind knowing we have her support.

What really stands out is how approachable and proactive the team is. They make complex legal matters feel manageable and are always available to guide us through anything that comes up. Over the years, they’ve become a trusted partner in our business, not just a service provider.

If you’re looking for a law firm that is dependable, knowledgeable, and genuinely invested in their clients, I highly recommend working with them.

The Long Law firm represented me for a civil suit and false restraining order that was put on me, and i am more than satisifed with the result from both cases. I would highly recommend them to anyone who's dealing with a civil case that they want handeld expeditiously in a professional manner.

I call this Law Firm, in need of internet search help for NYS government database site.
Mr. Jim Long was nice, nice enough to help me.

Knowing I wasn't in need of hiring an attorney @ this time. 
Very impressed with his professional manner.

I have a small business and was referred to Long Law by a client when I was dealing with a contract issue. Boy, am I thankful! In addition to navigating my legal issues, Jim was always thinking about what was best for my business in the long run. His creative solutions helped me to avoid court on two occasions, which was both a relief and kept my costs down. I hired Long Law to revise my client contracts to prevent further issues, and I continue to recommend the firm to colleagues. Thanks to Jim, Payton, and the rest of the team—you were worth every penny, and I can’t recommend you all highly enough.

I had a great experience working with this firm. Megan Thomas was professional, responsive, and explained everything clearly throughout the process. I truly appreciated the guidance and would highly recommend her to anyone needing legal help in the workplace.

Megan Thomas is the attorney you hire when you have been sexually harassed at work or experienced sexual assault.  She is an expert in this constantly evolving area of the law and provides a top-notch client experience.  Trust me you won't be disappointed.

Megan Thomas is an excellent attorney. She is practical, compassionate, and committed to doing what is right for her clients. She does not charge fees upfront, and fights to get her clients the best possible outcome, even if that sometimes means advising them not to move forward with a case. Megan helps people understand their rights and find closure. I highly recommend her.

I hired Megan after experiencing sexual harassment in the workplace, and would recommend her to anyone. She was direct, compassionate, and honest about what to expect. Megan was responsive, thorough, and always made time to answer my questions, even during tight deadlines. She helped me feel reassured during an incredibly difficult time and, helped me tremendously with my case.

I would personally like to take the time to recognize Megan.  I had a very personal and tragic situation happen to myself and my family.  Megan stepped right up and was more than willing to take the case she took it personally.  She made me feel like I had someone that was going to be there for me every step of the way.  She was diligent, confident, knowledgeable, compassionate and extremely professional.  She really protects her clients and makes sure she can get you everything you deserve and fights for you until the very end.  I can tell you from personal experience, I’m so glad I was fortunate enough to work with her.  She truly is wonderful.  5 stars isn’t enough to give her.  I can promise if you use Megan you will not be disappointed.
Megan Thomas is an incredibly skilled and knowledgeable trial attorney helping survivors of sexual harassment and pregnancy discrimination. I highly recommend her!

Megan Thomas is an excellent trial attorney.  She is very competent and empathetic.  I would recommend Megan Thomas Law for anyone looking for an attorney in New York to help with a sexual harassment or pregnancy discrimination case.
Megan Thomas is the premier employment and sexual abuse attorney in New York. Megan Thomas Law Firm is responsive and hardworking. I recommend this firm 10/10.
Megan is a fierce advocate and stops at nothing to obtain justice for her clients. Highly recommended!
Knowledgeable, responsive, professional, and always kept me updated! Her excellent,  no nonsense communications were always timely and she was patient with my questions and my concerns.

Megan handled my case very well. She helped me through a turbulent time in my life, and knowing she was there to protect my rights was invaluable. She was prompt and I always felt heard, overall I was very happy and impressed.

Megan Thomas provided me with exceptional legal counsel during a recent dispute with my employer. She is professional, attentive, and highly efficient. Thanks to her expertise, we reached a mutually desirable result with the employer quickly and on good terms. I am very grateful for her help and highly recommend her services.

It was fantastic working with Megan. She was able to make the best out of a challenging situation. I appreciate her honest assessment and responsiveness. I was particularly impressed with her strategic approach.

Megan was a calming presence and helped guide me through a very difficult experience. She was very knowledgeable and communicative, and I would highly recommend working with her to anyone in the future!

Working with Megan was a real pleasure. I went through a very difficult experience, and she was kind and compassionate. She was thorough in her work, and timely. She worked hard to get me good results, and then was sure to come in on her off time to make sure I got what I needed in a timely fashion. Choose her if you have any legal concerns.

Megan’s knowledge and experience not only saved me from being unfairly fired but also helped my voice by heard. Her depth of knowledge on the unfair treatment of women, moms and workplace discrimination saved my family and income.  Her dedication is unmatched.  Trustworthy and she takes on your struggle as if were her own.
Megan has been so extremely helpful, communicative and transparent while working together! It's evident she's highly knowledgeable, friendly and willing to take on any challenge. Highly recommend.
So thankful to have found Megan as my lawyer! She was amazing throughout the entire process and helped me get what I deserved. I would highly recommend.

Working with Megan was an outstanding experience from start to finish. She is not only incredibly professional and knowledgeable, but also warm, responsive, and truly committed to her clients' best interests. Megan's guidance was clear, thoughtful, and grounded in a deep understanding of the law. I felt confident and supported every step of the way.

If you’ve experienced workplace harassment, discrimination, retaliation for speaking up, or believe you were wrongfully terminated, I strongly recommend Megan Thomas Law. Megan is compassionate, knowledgeable, and truly dedicated to advocating for her clients - you’ll be in excellent hands.

I did most of the work in the closing of this house as he was not aggressive at all in pushing the seller and her attorney to get things done.

reviews.text: Dirk represented us in a civil case that took two years to resolve. He was always available and easy to talk to. We truly appreciate his kindness and willingness to explain every step along the way. He never once made us feel that a question was stupid or that our case was any less important despite being relatively small. It was consistently a pleasure working with him: With his encouragement and guidance we stood up for ourselves and fought.  Sometimes that's the most important thing of all.

My husband and I could not be happier with the results in our matter against a municipality represented by Dirk Oudemool. We parted ways with another attorney before we hired Dirk. If you are looking for an old-school generalist with a lot of experience who is willing to pick up the phone and chase people down in writing and in person to get things done for you, Dirk is your guy. If you want an attorney you can have immediate access to who returns your calls and keeps you in the loop about all he is doing to settle your matter, then Dirk is for you. If you are looking for slick and shiny corporate flash, go elsewhere. We will definitely turn to Dirk to handle any additional matters, whether mundane or complicated, in the future.

Straight forward, experienxed attorney who was honest with me about my case and always concerned that the financial costs of fighting may be more than it is worth.   He will tell you when it is better to cut your losses and walk away.   Dirk considers the stress the court process causes his client and weighs that in to advising what should be done.

I have hired Dirk to handle both civil cases and family court.  We have been to Supreme Court together.   I would not pick anyone else to be sitting next to me at the table in front of the Judge.  He is professional and makes himself available to take your calls.  His paralegal has been working for him for 28 years.

I then went to court for my brother just to see the person who killed him was still a free man , I lost control and beat the guy up pretty bad. With that being said bill is a family friend of 10+ years he then contacted my mother and begged her to let him represent me , so he showed up to the jail for my court date and told me he would help me out. He then with held 7,500  dollars from me and swore I would sit for seven years unless I paid him which I already then felt was a sense of entrapment because he already showed up to the first court date . Bill was very unresponsible i live in Albany New York on 4 different counts i drove to Syracuse for court dates and he never showed up which then adjourned my court dates. He was very selfish he used my mother’s relationship to take advantage of me many times in the sense of not caring at all and just asking for money . Also in this time he was representing me on a injury case where I only was giving 10 thousand out of the 25 thousand and he clearly screwed me out of that as well because I later found out he was only suppose to take a third. Yes I know if screwed me once he was gonna do it again but I was in the hospital for my injury and needed help and he took 70 percent of my injury money for him self , I thought he would of been a better man than that seeing how my mother was friends with him for 10+ years and served him at blue tusk till the day they closed down (rip Mike) . Bill is not professional he does not make any contact I had to call his assistant for EVERY COURT DATE by checking up on the case casually. I was even arrested once because he never called me for a court date and I had a warrant I was unaware of and the only thing bill could say is “sorry” . I don’t understand how people like him exist especially seeing how he claimed to be a friend and was actually just using my family for the publicity and money .
bill you are probably the best lawyer in syracuse thank you so much for taking care of me and my brother and my father I couldn't be more grateful

This includes considering what is the best way to approach a case with the resources (money) your client can afford.  Andrew has both of these skills.  All lawyers do not have them. reviews.text: Most of us only seek lawyers as a matter of last resort, to salvage something important that we are near to losing or have abruptly lost.  If it's your livelihood, your job, Satter Ruhlen is the place to go.  I consulted Sarah Ruhlen when I was overwhelmed and felt betrayed. I felt better as soon as she started responding to my problem. She delivers her expertise frankly and with compassion. She validated the injustice and then moved right on to envisioning a rational resolution.  She does not promise to heal the moral injury or secure revenge.  She knows the law and explains its limits clearly and kindly. With her wise counsel and service to the truth, Sarah Ruhlen helps you reframe your vision of the path you are now on, which by necessity will be different from the one you were on when the workplace situation arose that brought you to her door. Her goal is to anchor you with reasoned strategy, to be good company as you learn to take care of yourself now that stuff has happened. She firmly establishes realistic and safe steps you can take to regain some stability and a rational outlook for the future.  The steps she counsels are safe because you know you can count on her knowledge of the law, how it has been interpreted with precedence and her distillation of her expertise and experience as it applies to your particular problem.  Everything she predicted as an aftermath of my painful incident came to pass. Every option she suggested yielded positive outcomes. You can trust the Satter Ruhlen law firm.
reviews.ownerResponse: Janet, thank you so much for these kind words. This is why we do this work. I am so honored to have been able to help you move through that difficult time and so thrilled that you are thriving now!

reviews.text:
My family used Mackenzie Hughes since the 1970s. They were expensive but reliable. Not so now. My wife and I engaged the firm to put together our will and estate planning. Over the course of months we worked with an attorney. She was not skilled in communicating complex legal matters to university educated lay people, throwing out arcane legal jargon and showing an inability to explain things in plain English. Worse, she was abrupt and lecturing at times. In short, she lacked the personality and social skills to deal with the public. It got even worse. I had lined up the trust department of a regional bank to take on trustee obligations and put them in direct touch with our lawyer. After paying Mackenzie Hughes thousands of dollars and too many hours of our time, the bank officer, months later, informed us that they could not take us on as a client because of the way our lawyer had organized our estate planning, which didn't meet their standards. Rather than expend more money and precious time trying to get the lawyer to get things right with the bank, I gave up, too furious to have to return to dealing with an unpleasant person who seemed not to be able to do her job right.

After two years of sulking, on the recommendation of a financial planner, we engaged another top Syracuse law firm to start the process all over again from scratch. They were familiar with the obstreperous Mackenzie Hughes attorney with whom we had worked, noting her reputation was well known in the legal community. In commiseration with our negative experience, this new firm gave us a discount on what they charge. Their assigned attorney and her paralegal are outstanding in how they explain things. Moreover, they have organized our estate planning in a much improved way compared with the Mackenzie Hughes attorney.

Bottom line is this: I have terminated a 50-year family association with Mackenzie Hughes. To use a legal term: caveat emptor.
Very knowledgeable in all aspects of the law. The very best law firm in central New York.
An excellent firm with a stellar reputation. I am always pleased when I refer clients here or use their services myself.

reviews.text: MCV Law- My case was crazy I originally hurt my neck in 2010. My Doctor told me it would never be the same so I adjusted my life around my injury. Quit bowling, softball etc so that I would not make matters worse. In 2016 I exasperated my neck injury and had to get an attorney to amend everything together so I could get treatment. That’s when I meant Christopher Stringham and he went to work and got everything amended. I got treatment while still working. In 12/04/18 I fell in my trailer moving a 3000lb pallet my feet came right out from under me landing on my left elbow and buttock. This tore up my shoulder and destroyed my C6 in neck. After constant delays and denials I finally got my surgeries. The law office working for the carrier did everything they could to prevent everything from happening but Christopher rode them hard always getting the edge. We tried settling and the law office for the carrier did nothing finally after almost 6 years the carrier fired the law firm and hired a new law firm to represent them. Christopher was always ahead of the game and when the new law firm was hired he went to town. We had are settlement done in about 6 months. We have sent a few clients over to them for consultation. Very professional , courteous , knowledgeable and we will keep sending people that need help over to them. Thank you again sincerely Kevin C
reviews.ownerResponse: Kevin, thank you so much for sharing your experience. We’re glad to hear that Christopher Stringham and our team were able to help you navigate such a long and challenging case and ultimately reach a successful settlement. Your recommendation means a great deal to us, and we’re honored that you continue to refer others to MCV Law for help.

reviews.text: Very professional and knowledgeable about my case. Explained, in detail, everything so that I understood what was going on at all times. I would highly recommend MCV Law to anyone.

reviews.text:
From the very first conversation, MCV Law made me feel heard — truly heard — for the first time. They were effective, and incredibly supportive throughout the entire process. What stood out the most was how they treated me with respect and compassion, not just as a case number but as a real person with a voice that mattered.

MCV Law is for the little people — the everyday individuals who often feel overlooked or powerless in the system. They fight to make things fair and honest, and they do it with integrity, skill, and heart. Their team moved fast, kept me informed, and made sure I understood every step along the way.

If you’re looking for a law firm that stands up for what’s right and makes you feel like you truly matter, MCV Law is the one. I can’t thank them enough.

reviews.text:
The MCV law firm were amazing. I was referred to them by a close friend who had a positive  SSDI outcome. Ill be honest, I had no idea what I was doing or expecting from this process. I contacted Karen Lowe(my lawyer)and she took it from there. I  had left my job, RN,  of 23 years  after having a left sided hip pain, for over 2.5 yes. This pain left me helpless. I was at my wits end ...I had no income for over two and half years. I finally had received a diagnosis of severe left hip osteoarthritis; my hip was bone on bone. Karen  was such an empathetic listener. Karen was well versed  with the medical history and presented the case to the judge,demonstarting how my condition prevented work.She explained my options and worked tirelessly to obtain a positive outcome.  Her staff, Perry Wilson and Haley Drabot , maintained constant  contact with me . They were responsive, answered my questions,and provided case updates.
The SSDI claim was approved . Karen Lowe at the MCV Law firm are highly recommended for those facing disability applications.
The kindness and expertise exhibited by Karen and her team were greatly appreciated. Thank you MCV LawFirm.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Belinda, for your wonderful review! We're so glad to hear that Karen Lowe and the team were able to support you through the process and achieve a positive outcome. Your recommendation means a lot to us!

reviews.ownerResponse: This client entered into a flat fee arrangement with us. We fully earned that fee pursuant to the terms of the retainer agreement that this client (who also has a law degree) entered into knowingly and voluntarily. She was quite satisfied with our services as evidenced by her initial 5-star review from the first tenant we successfully evicted for her. Only after we successfully accomplished her goal of evicting a second tenant that had not paid her, without a protracted litigation process, did she come to us demanding a refund for services that were rendered. Not only did we accomplish her goal of removing the bad tenant, we saved her an incalculable amount of money on lost revenue that would have been incurred during the time it took the court process to run its course and have the Sheriff ultimate remove the tenant. We have offered her a reasonable refund amount as a courtesy, however, there was no "unused portion" of a fee as she alleges. Her modified review is nothing more than attempt to obtain a refund of fees she is not due.

Extended non-responsiveness and failure to advance the case.

Galli Law agreed to represent me in October 2023. More than two years and four months later, there has been virtually no progress beyond the initial filing and request for documentation. The firm accepted this matter as a trade for IT services.

Communication has been consistently inadequate. In August 2025, I was told depositions would be scheduled, after which the firm became unresponsive for approximately six months. Multiple follow-up emails and monthly outreach by my assistant went unanswered.

There were repeated changes in personnel assigned to my case, requiring repeated re-explanations of case details. On the advice of another attorney, I requested that a mechanic’s lien and lis pendens be filed. Emails sent on December 4, 2025, and December 20, 2025, received no response.

October 2025, two years into the representation, I formally raised concerns about the lack of scheduled court dates or depositions. I was told the firm was “positioning for depositions” and asked to remain patient. Three additional months have passed, and no depositions have been scheduled.

Depositions typically occur during discovery within months of filing. A delay of more than two years without meaningful action or communication is unreasonable. Based on my experience, I cannot recommend this firm.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for your review. We vehemently disagree with and absolutely contest your characterization.

Our fee arrangements are set out in our written engagement terms, and we do not take matters as a ‘trade’ for services. We made a special arrangement with you due to your dire financial situation and fragile emotional state, but the value of legal services you've received far outweighs any "IT services" you exchanged. The contingency arrangement we made with you gives us an excellent incentive to progress your case - we don't get paid unless and until you do.

Not every requested filing is legally available. Mechanic’s liens are subject to strict statutory deadlines (which you were already past when you contacted us), and a notice of pendency (lis pendens) is only appropriate in cases that affect title to, or the right to possess, real property. When a filing is not legally permissible or not supported by the governing law, we cannot ethically file it on request, as is the situation in your case. Any attorney who advised you otherwise is incorrect.

Litigation also does not move on a television timeline. Court calendars, procedural rules, opposing-party conduct, and discovery obligations control scheduling, including depositions. You must also understand that you are not our only client and we need to align your case not only with our schedule, but also opposing counsels' schedules.

Again, we're very sorry you're not happy with the services you've been provided, but we have handled your case with appropriate care and diligence.

I had a bad experience hiring the services of the lawyer Jose Pérez.  I hired him to carry out the process that must be done after getting married.  Forms I-485, I-30 and I-765 had to be sent, and each one had certain requirements, I put my entire trust in limiting myself only to what the lawyer asked me because I assumed that if I hired a lawyer to carry  The process is because he had to know all the information that was required, so I gave the lawyer all the documents that he requested. During the process of waiting for a response from immigration, the lawyer did not receive calls, he only answered a text message 3 days later, he did not even inform me that an immigration letter had come to his office, which months later  I heard. After 6 months I received the denial of the I-485 forms because a document called affidavit was not sent, which the lawyer did not request and which said very clearly in the requirements that if it were not sent they could deny the case.  Here I clarify that I read the requirements after I was denied the case, because once again I affirm that the idea of ​​hiring the lawyer was because I did not consider myself with the necessary knowledge to carry out the process on my own. So I basically paid him to fill out the forms because he didn't even know what documents were required, they even denied me the I-765 form too. I lost time and money!  I would not recommend the lawyer José Pérez and his team under any circumstances.  lousy customer service.

I know that you or your office do not control the approval or denial of the applications, what you could control and for what you hired it was to verify that the requirements that immigration requests for the form I-485 were complete.
Immigration clearly states that the reason the petition was denied is because the form I-864 (affidavit) was not included. It is not true that it was denied due to a change in the public charge policy, the public charge policy came into effect on February 24, 2020 and my application was sent in September 2019. What is true is that due to the fact that immigration denied form I - 485, I must make a new application and obviously I must adhere to that policy, what which means that I must pay the form fee again and add form I-944.
I have several reasons why I did not accept your appeal proposal:
1. I don't trust your work
2. the estimated response times of the appeal and adjustment of status are the same, so I risked making the appeal and waiting 6 months or more and that the appeal would not work in my favor, because clearly in the instructions to fill out the The form says that one of the requirements is to send the affidavit and if it is not sent they could deny the case, and I did not send it, then by law they are right, that way I would lose more time; On the other hand, if I applied for the I-485 again, but sending the complete requirements, I was going to wait the same 6 months for the response.
3. I Hired the services of another attorney.
For the services provided, I paid 3,230 as follows: 1,500. (One thousand five hundred dollars to start the process and 1,730 (one thousand seven hundred and thirty dollars) to send the documents.
The I-130 petition is in force, it is true and the fee is $ 535, that is, I pay $ 2,695 for this request! How much more do you want me to pay you?
I am very clear that you are not competent and the reasons why I did not want you to continue representing me, my intention with this comment is not that you try to wash your hands affirming things that are not, what I want is that the People who read it know why I do not recommend it.
reviews.ownerResponse:
I am really sorry that you are not happy with our services. Interestingly, you left many things out. For instance, this office cannot control the approval or denial of forms by USCIS, specially yours, that the reason it was denied was because it fell during the public charge policy change and the i-864 had been changed because of that. Also, you failed to mention that I offered you to file the appeal at no cost to you because we believed USCIS wrongly denied the petition. Despite the fact that we offered you several times to fix that issue, you said you did not want us to and asked us to withdraw. Also, you did not even paid for the services rendered, which you indicate we did not fulfill our services...but still you are using the i-130 petition we filed on you behalf. So, that was part of our service. We explained to you that the fastest way to communicate with our office was via text and if by phone 48 hours call back and you were explained that at the time of the interview. We always communicated with you within that timeframe. In fact, there were several issues with you not communicating and USCIS sending back letters they have sent to you and you were the one not fixing your address problem which we told you many times to do given the return of documents from USCIS.

Again, you were given the option several times to let us handle the denial and you did not give us the opportunity to do so. It was your choice. You did not lose money because you never paid us fully. As far as the fee paid to USCIS, you chose not let us get you the approval and you are still using the i-130 filed by us.

I am sorry you were not satisfied and wish you the best of luck. We are not going to pursue collection against you for your failure to pay our office anyways.

This review is based on my direct experience in litigation where Michael Murphy represented McDonald’s.
My complaint was filed on December 30, 2024. McDonald’s had a clear deadline of March 1, 2025 to respond. That deadline passed without a response. Instead, an extension was later granted moving the deadline to April 11, 2025, despite the fact that I did not authorize or consent to any extension, which unnecessarily delayed the case.
Since then, settlement negotiations have stalled, even though McDonald’s previously made an $7,000 settlement offer. From my perspective, progress stopped once delays were introduced, and the strategy shifted to prolonging litigation rather than resolving it.
For an individual litigant facing a large corporate defense, this type of delay tactic is exhausting and damaging. Deadlines matter. Consent matters. Good-faith negotiation matters.
If you are dealing with this attorney, do not assume timelines will be honored, insist on everything in writing, and monitor every deadline yourself. Expect a defense strategy focused on delay rather than resolution.

reviews.text: This is just in reference of one of the tenant lawyers who advocates for slum lords. We already knew we had legal grounds to break a lease based on our own research and advice from the union, but wanted to get a lawyer's opinion about it. He kept shrugging his shoulders when we presented him with all our data showing proof that our landlord was breaking the law. Then he proceeded to gaslight us about rental conditions, stating that we would be lucky to find a place that met basic standards of the warrant of habitability unless we wanted to pay $5000 a month in Syracuse. Rest assured we found a better lawyer who confirmed our thoughts and process. We also found a better place and, of course, did not have to pay that much. I am just writing this so people do not get discouraged by crap advice and settle for less than what they are paying for. Check in with the Tenant's Right's Union and a different lawyer if anyone tries to make you believe differently when you are being treated unfairly. Not standing up for yourself because of poor counsel or lack of knowledge is how slum lords keep getting away with what they are doing.

reviews.text:
VLP provides access to legal assistance to individuals and families who might otherwise have no access to justice, particularly in civil legal matters.  They are incredible!

The staff at VLP are organized and supportive, providing volunteers with resources and guidance needed to help clients. Their commitment to pro bono work helps people in vulnerable situations, and also fosters a sense of community among the legal professionals involved.

If you are a paralegal, attorney, or community member looking for an opportunity to contribute positively, Voluntary Lawyers Project is an outstanding organization making a realtime impact.

VLP is an organization where you can contribute your focus, time, and other resources and you will see and feel lives change in profound and meaningful ways.

reviews.text: I reached out to Paul for help with a grandparents' rights case after the tragic loss of our daughter, which left us struggling to maintain a connection with our granddaughter. A friend recommended Paul, and from our very first meeting, we were impressed by his professionalism and honesty. He didn’t sugarcoat anything but explained the legal process, what evidence we’d need, and the likelihood of success. Thanks to his expertise, we now have a court-approved visitation schedule, and we are slowly reestablishing our bond with our granddaughter. Paul was exactly the kind of support we needed during such a difficult time, and we are incredibly thankful for his help.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for taking the time to share your experience. - I was significantly over-billed (the retainer that I paid was not applied) and I did not receive an invoice that was an important part of the billing history. When I asked about it, I was told that they had sent a prior billing statement (which I did not receive). When I asked that a copy of the communication to be forwarded to me, I did not receive it and had to follow up again.  I never received the original communication that they had supposedly sent, but they did send a new invoice that credited me for the retainer.

- When I asked for an estimate on how much time it would take to complete a topic, I was told that it would take 1hr to complete.  After I requested that they not spend any more than 1hr total on the topic, they charged me for 2hrs of time for that topic.  When I followed up to explain that I was expecting to be charged for 1hr based on the prior communication regarding the topic, they only partially credited me for the extra time that I was charged for.

reviews.text:
I can’t say enough great things about Stanley Law Offices and especially Megan Fallon. After suffering a serious wrist injury that ultimately required surgery, I knew I needed strong representation—and that’s exactly what I got.
From day one, Megan was incredibly responsive and available throughout the entire process. No matter how long the case took (and yes, it was about two years), she was always there to answer questions, provide updates, and make sure I understood what was happening at every stage. I never felt left in the dark.
What I appreciated most was her direct, no-sugarcoating approach. Megan told me what I needed to hear—not just what I wanted to hear. She kept me grounded, realistic, and focused, which made a huge difference in staying on track during a long and sometimes stressful process. Her honesty built trust, and I always knew she was looking out for my best interests.
This wasn’t a quick case, but Megan left no stone unturned. She was thorough, detail-oriented, and relentless in making sure every aspect of my injury and recovery was fully represented.    That level of dedication gave me confidence that my case was being handled the right way.
If you’re looking for a law firm that truly fights for you and an attorney who will be honest, accessible, and committed from start to finish, I highly recommend Stanley Law Offices—and especially Megan Fallon. Five stars all the way.
reviews.ownerResponse: Drew, we can't thank you enough for being so straightforward and taking this time and energy to share your worker's compensation experience with Attorney Megan Fallon, the entire WC Team at Stanley Law and all of us here at Stanley Law Offices. To hear first-hand that you always felt taken care of, communicated clearly with, and treated with respect the respect you deserve, is exactly what we strive for each and every time. We so appreciate your true story, and your partnership in your case. Thank you again for the kind recommendation! Sharing this with Megan and our entire team!

After the lawyer kept me on the fence for a week she left me a voicemail telling me that she won’t take my case. reviews.text: IF YOU WANT THE MAXIMUM WARD FOR YOUR COMP SETTLEMENT DO NOT HIRE STANLEY LAW FIRM. THEY WILL TAKE MONEY OUT OF YOUR CHECK. CANNOT EXPLAIN WHAT IT IS FOR OR WHERE IT GOES. THEY WILL NOT ABSOLUTELY NOT GET YOU YOUR MAX SETTLEMENT. I would NEVER hire these people ever again. They screwed my husband so BAD in his workers comp case. Had more paralegals than he knew what to do with, never returned calls, didn't do paper work correct, didn't fight for all his injuries. We're taking money out of his checks, and cannot tell us why "leans against awards" it was called. They def NOT work for him. Stanley law tells you what you want to hear. To make you hire them. They fail to say they work in conjunction with a lot of the workers compensation companies. They make you think there working for you. They should be turned into the BAR ASSOCIATION. After several attempts to speak with Joe Stanley himself. I never even met the man. He's the person I hired not everybody else. Which we will be doing. Run while you can hire someone honest. With my husbands injuries he should have got way more they MADE him settle for less, and never went after the owner of the company. Told us we had no case. Then check around it it was out of the statute to limitations. Because I believe them...
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback - once again. This case dates back many years. While we cannot discuss specific cases or clients publicly due to confidentiality and professional responsibility, we can assure you that all matters brought to our office are reviewed thoroughly and handled in accordance with both legal standards and our firm's values.

Please also note based on the claimants' spouse's comment above, that any Liens are a legal part of the process, having nothing to do with Stanley Law, but rather outside organizations who've placed a lien on the claimant's potential award(s) in their case(s). It is then any law firms' legal responsibility to satisfy those liens PRIOR to a claimant receiving their award. We must follow the law.

As represented in the hundreds of other five star reviews across our many offices, you can see that our team strives to communicate decisions and processes clearly and to support individuals in finding the resources they need.

We remain committed to listening, learning, and serving with integrity.

reviews.text: Only Lawyer I've ever met that actually calls back and follows up on everything you send. EXTREMELY responsive. I actually called maybe 20 different lawyers and started processing with 2 of those 20. What I thought was going to be another week or 2, he completed within 24 hours.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks, Tony! I know lawyers are known for not returning calls very promptly, which is why I do my best to always stay in touch with my clients, and keep them informed on how long it will take to get things done.  Thank you for your business, and I look forward to working with you in the future!

reviews.text: Working with Matt has been great! He Uses appropriate tone and language to  provide relevant updates and summarize information that makes it easy to understand!

reviews.text: Koldin Law Center is amazing! The experience and integrity of the entire Koldin team have earned it a reputation of excellence and fairness with government agencies. I am grateful every day that we took advantage of the expertise of Koldin Law Center.

Scott assisted my family and I with our estate planning and was extremely professional and thorough. He made us feel very comfortable and put our minds at ease. We could not be more satisfied with the high levels of professionalism from the Koldin Law Center.

They are the experts in their field. Other Lawyers look to them for second opinions! I couldn’t be happier at how professional and knowledgeable the whole staff is. They truly care about individuals and families.
Very rude and condescending. Would not recommend.
Our family started working with Koldin and Lavine in 1994. They have been trusted advisors and got my parents wishes to be realized. We appreciate everything they helped us with.

He is a genuine, honest man who takes time to make sure everyone involved understands each step, a true master of his craft. If your looking for trustworthy and knowledgeable legal services, this place is it. Thank you for all that you’ve done for my family!
Really good experience, very professional and responsive.

Koldin Law Center was referred to me by a family member to assist my father in his estate/trust/probate planning. My father was a resident of NYS for over 65 years. His business, assets, family, etc all still reside in NYS. He pays yearly income taxes, property taxes, etc... STILL to NYS. When I called and tried to set up an appointment with Koldin Law Firm, I was told he had to do it in his state of residency- where he lives most of the year. Because he is a "snowbird" as of recently, I was told that there was no way Koldin Law Center could handle his planning. This was totally acceptable- rules are rules. However, the way the secretary spoke to me was unprofessional, rude, and borderline degrading. I had a few simple questions about why this was the rule and wanted to be sure I was clear about my father still maintaining a business and paying taxes here. He isn't retired or in a nursing home out of state. He simply spends his winters down south. I thought maybe she misunderstood me. I truly didn't understand why my father would have to file this paperwork out of state, when 99.9% of his wealth is in NY. Instead of showing kindness and clarifying my questions, the secretary accused me of "trying to get around the law" and was incredibly condescending. My questions were things like "even if he pays taxes to NYS?" and "even if his business is here?" I was truly confused as this was the first I'd ever heard of such a thing. I was not even remotely close to looking for some sort of loophole! I would NEVER ever recommend this firm to ANYONE after I was treated that way, regardless of what legal work they need done.
Top notch for wills, and estate planning.
It is easy to work with them. Great services and great communication. Highly recommend.

He came to their support group several times pro bono and gave talks about estate planning/elder law for patients and families who were facing catastrophic disability from a terrible disease. Exceptional service for will and power of attorney needs! This attorney went above and beyond, guiding me through the process with expertise and care. Highly recommend for their professionalism and commitment. A definite 5-star experience!

reviews.text: Mr. Dombrow was contacted regarding a debt collection issue. While unable to assist in court due to scheduling, he was a wealth of information, non-judgmental and so easy to speak with. You’d be lucky to have his firms services.

My husband and I bought a car from a dealership in Watertown in March of 2023 as we were expecting our second baby in June. 2 weeks after we bought the car the engine light came on, they “fixed” the issue multiple times. It took us 3 months of bringing the car in and waiting hours before they even offered a loaner vehicle. By the 5th month we decided to look for legal help. As the dealership wasn’t listening to us and continued to give us a hard time. We contacted legal aid in our county who told us our best bet was to file a lawsuit. I contacted a law firm( I can’t remember the name) who said it was not something they did, however they knew of someone and gave me the number to Dombrow Law firm.  I contacted Dombrow law firm because my family worked hard to get a newer vehicle for our expanding family. I spent life being walked on by others and letting it go, and I felt the dealership thought they could do the same and get away with selling a car with a major issue. I never had to hire Dombrow law firm as he was able to give me advice on what to say to the dealership in hopes that they would give us a different vehicle or take the car and loan back. After 7 months of this issue with our car and finally flat out telling the dealership what we wanted they allowed us to get a different car. This impacted my life more than I think the attorney knows. He helped me realize I can’t keep letting people or places walk over me. This situation helped me gain confidence to stand up when I feel it’s needed. To voice my opinion.  Something that’s valuable to have and I hope to teach my little girls. I know in the future if I have any car issues Dombrow Law firm will be my first call! He most definitely did not have to help me to the extent he did without us hiring him. Coming from a low income home my husband and I are forever grateful for this. Even though we would of found the funds if needed so that the dealership would not get away with it. I truly recommend Dombrow Law firm for anyone’s legal needs involving a car. Thank you!

Contacted Dombrow Law Firm about internet scamming situation.  Within a week I had a phone interview with Russell Dombrow himself.  I was able to tell my story without being rushed and am now awaiting referrals from Russell for a lawyer in California who can represent me.  I have been searching for help since April and have spoken to at least 10 other law firms and legal aid associations.  I even had an attorney from California laugh at me and wish me "good luck!"  Russell Dombrow is that rare individual:  an attorney who cares.

Unprofessional!  Sends threatening emails.  Speaks down to you.  Files were sent, claims the information is not there, but it is.  Angry, nasty man.  Nice staffers, he has less to be desired.  Acts like he has a splinter he can't remove during each interaction.
Russell Dombrow is the best attorney! He is worth every cent. A real fighter B⁠-⁠)

I would definitely recommend this firm, and I would absolutely use them in the future.

It turns out that my Consumer law problem was resolved to my satisfaction without having to go through legal channels, but knowing that I would have Dombrow in my corner was a great comfort.

The intake/ review process was thorough, professional and couteous. The legal assistant I spoke with generous with his time, listened to my concerns carefully, and responded promptly.

It was a very positive experience.

Mr Russel and Adrian were pretty good with follow-ups,m. I had a consultation with Mr. Russel about a case that I needed clarifications with and maybe opening a case, he was very knowledgeable and patient, and very very helpful, explaining and clarifying my questions. We actually for this case didn’t ended up taking it to the court.
Thank you so much Russel.

Very unprofessional. I called to inquire about representation. Spoke to Russell and gave him the proposal for my legal situation. He told me that he would represent me. The case was against Crouse hospital. I emailed him all my information to get the ball rolling. He never got back to me until 2 years later asking me do I still need representation. By this time the hospital had garnished my wages and I never got the representation which initially was to stop them from illegally suing me. I wouldn’t refer anyone to this firm as it left a pretty bad image by the way my case was mishandled

reviews.text: Very helpful, knowledgeable and professional. Recommend wholeheartedly.

Finding the right lawyer is a daunting task. Mr. Russell Dombrow
( Dombrow Law Firm ) is very knowledgeable and dedicated to the practice
of law. He will analyze a small or large case and promptly respond with his
honest opinion. I was lucky to find Russell.  Dombrow Law Firm

If you are shopping for an attorney and in a desperate situation, financially. Move along....You need to find a lawyer that will help you, not steal your money with no real services rendered.

Id love a job where i could charge the client without actually filing with the court! Thats free money. Oh yeah, there are thieves on both sides!
I have known Mr. Dombrow professionally for several years. I would not hesitate to refer an upstate client to him.

Russell is very efficient and thorough.  He takes the time to explain any information that is confusing.  Russell is highly detail oriented.  He researches topics if he needs to get a clearer understanding to a particular topic.  I am extremely comfortable using Russell as my family's lawyer.  He is professional and respectful to clients judges and others he comes in contact with.

My only negative comment, is: it is sometimes difficult to get a return message from Russell.  My preferred method of contact is email and there have been times when I have waited quite a few days (and even more than a week) for a return email.  For the most part, this issue has not created any major problem and Russell does always reply.

I highly recommend Dombrow Law Firm for legal services.

My experience with Dombrow Law Firm was a positive one. Mr. Dombrow is a man of integrity and compassion; he is also a hardworking and knowledgeable attorney. I recommend Dombrow Law Firm to anyone who is in need of legal representation.

reviews.text:
I cannot in good conscience recommend Caterina.
I hired her for help in amending my grandfather’s name for my Italian dual citizenship application.
Throughout the process, not only was she was incredibly unresponsive, but she also ended up charging me significantly more than originally quoted.
During our first call, I had a sense that she was not totally listening to me – and when she ended up charging me more money *after* I signed, it became clear that was the case.
Needless to say, I felt duped.
As far as her unresponsiveness - I hired a lawyer to remove stress from the application process, but trying to track her down caused *more* stress. Weeks on end without an answer even though I made it very clear that time was of the utmost importance.

In the end, I got my grandfather’s birth certificate, and my passport just arrived a few weeks ago, so the process proved successful and she clearly knows what she is doing.

But if you are in any sort of time crunch – avoid!!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your feedback. We value all our clients and we seek to find resolutions to all problems. I see it has been over a year since your matter was concluded. In order to obtain Irish Citizenship, I needed assistance obtaining the "long form”, certified copy of my deceased mother’s birth record from New York State.  Because of New York State privacy rules, and my mother being deceased, there was no way for me to obtain this  birth record without getting a court order.  Caterina proved to be particularly skilled in navigating the inner workings of the bureaucracies involved.  Additionally,  she was straight forward with me as to what steps I would need to take in assisting her.  She was clear, concise, caring and professional in all of our interactions. reviews.ownerResponse: In my over 20 years of experience handling Social Security claims I can tell you that I have seen far too many cases where people thought that they could file themselves just to get a rejection because they made mistakes in the application process. Worse still, those mistakes on the front end of a claim can tarnish the case throughout the entire process and ultimately result in a claim being rejected throughout the appeals process. As an attorney I can not give details about a particular case. We value an individual's privacy and do not communicate with third parties, even if those third parties are spouses or parents seeking information about a claim. We also do not communicate through e-mail as not all services are secure. Even though many people would prefer that we do these things, we do not for their own protection.  As far as fees are concerned, our fee arrangement is dictated to us by the Social Security Administration. For work performed in front of the agency attorney fees must be contingent upon a successful outcome and limited to 25% of back-due benefits.  This is significantly lower than most personal injury lawyers who charge between 33% and 40% for their work. Further, many attorneys do not accept claims at the application stage because the back-due benefits are typically very low and there is significant work involved. We accept claims at this level in order to avoid the types of problems I've mentioned above. It may seem like when we are able to win a claim at the application stage that we haven't done much work, but that is because much of our work is done behind the scenes and is tailored to laying the groundwork for an appeal if necessary.

I’ve worked with attorneys before, but Ronda is on another level. She’s sharp, fearless, and fully committed to protecting her client. While reviewing my real estate documents, she caught something that had no business being in there — something everyone else missed. That could’ve cost me big, but not on her watch.

She doesn’t play games. She doesn’t take shortcuts. And she absolutely does not take shit from anyone trying to pull a fast one. Ronda is a beast in the best way — firm when needed, always prepared, and crystal clear in communication.

If you want a real estate lawyer who will go to war to protect your interests and make sure everything is done right, Ronda is who you want in your corner. I trust her with my life, my property, and my peace of mind.

I’m beyond grateful to have her guiding this process.
Thank you, Ronda. You’re a fing legend.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you so much for this incredible review! Your words truly mean a lot to us. We are deeply committed to protecting our clients’ interests, and we’re so glad our attention to detail and determination made a real impact on your experience.

We’re honored by the trust you placed in us and proud to have guided you through such an important process. Knowing you felt protected and supported is exactly what we strive for with every client.

Thank you again for sharing your experience & best of luck on your new beginning!

Best wishes,
Ronda Akl & the Akl & Associates Team

reviews.text:
Last fall, I was under contract to purchase a house that turned out to be bad in multiple ways and my attorney at the time was doing nothing to help me. I called multiple firms trying to hire new representation but no one would even talk to me because I was already under contract. The ONLY firm that helped was Ronda's office. At the time, I was unsure of who I was I was speaking with because I called so many places. The gentleman gave me solid pointers, worthy advice, wished me the best of luck, and seemed genuinely sincere when he said he wished there was more he could do for me. I eventually managed to get out of that contract and found another house. I had to search dates to narrow down when I had called all those firms to figure out who it was that helped me when no one else would. I came to learn is was one of Ronda's son who helped me.

I told them I was purchasing another house and because he helped me when no one else would, I would like them to represent me for this new closing. From the get-go, they were on the ball and showed a level of care, concern, and professionalism absent of my previous attorney. Robin in particular, was an invaluable asset and an immense help with keeping things on track when hiccoughs occurred. Ronda and her staff are so efficient and detail-oriented that they found multiple issues in the title work that were overlooked in the previous sale of the house. The seller's attorney was their representation when they bought the house and had missed glaring issues in the survey details, encroachments, and parcel additions completely omitted from the formal description. Ronda's office found them all and had them corrected as quickly as possible.

I received sound advice on what to accept and what to push for with regard to inspection, septic, water, etc. The entire transaction left me feeling like FINALLY someone had my back! I felt truly protected having them at the helm. Things went wrong in ways I couldn't have fathomed, yet their experience and expertise navigated those issues with ease.

They worked hand-in-hand with my lender to be sure all the necessary paperwork and documentation were in order and in a timely fashion. Robin went above and beyond when time constraints loomed and closing was going to be delayed by factors having nothing to do with our side of the transaction. She stayed on top of everything and in the end, I had a smooth closing in under 60 days.

I am beyond grateful for Ronda, her sons, and Robin. I am so glad I decided to go back and find the one person who was willing to help me when no one else would. That spoke volumes about their character and what followed was consistent and did not disappoint. I highly recommend Akl and Assoc.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you so much for your heartfelt and detailed review. Your journey and kind words truly moved us. We’re so glad you found your way back to our office and that we were able to support you through such an important milestone. It means the world to us to know that the advice you received early on made a lasting impact and that our team — especially Robin and my son — could guide you through the rest of the process with the care and attention you deserved.

We always strive to ensure our clients feel supported, protected, and prioritized, and it’s incredibly rewarding to hear that this came through during your experience. Thank you for your trust and for taking the time to share your story. We’re honored to have been part of your homebuying journey and truly appreciate your recommendation.

Warmest wishes,
Ronda Akl & Team
Akl & Associates PLLC

Arranging care for an elderly parent is difficult and emotional enough...but when you add the complexities of obtaining long-term care into the mix, the stress can be overwhelming.

My family sought Anthony's legal assistance in two separate instances. Several years ago, he set up a Family Asset Protection Trust, which successfully allowed my mother to qualify for Community Medicaid shortly after she was admitted to an Assisted Living Program. More recently, he helped us navigate the limited options available to us when Nursing Home-level care became necessary, which in turn required an abrupt pivot in financial planning.

Throughout both experiences, Anthony offered useful guidance. He patiently responded to my many emailed questions, and was prompt in delivering legal documents when needed. He is knowledgeable in the field of Elder Law, and has experience with practical aspects of the NY state Medicaid program. I would recommend the Marrone Law Firm to anyone tasked with making difficult decisions on behalf of a loved one, as well as to those with the foresight to engage in timely estate planning, no matter how small the "estate" might be.

One of the takeaways I gained from this experience, including the legal insights gleaned from Anthony, is just how significant the potential benefits of pre-planning for long term care can be, if undertaken early enough. With rising costs of care and a tsunami of baby boomers about to enter advanced old age, addressing these issues will become even more critical. Yet as Teddy Roosevelt observed,
"Old age is like everything else. To be
successful at it you've got to start young."
Everyone was pleasant and friendly but also really well informed and efficient. Small business personalized care with big business efficiency. Highly recommend
Very responsive, professional and pleasant to speak with. Would definitely recommend to family and friends.

They did my mothers will so that there was only one executor when there should have been two executives per my mom's instructions when they were called. They didn't want to talk about anything. All they wanted was a consultation fee. That's my experience and it wasn't a good one

To reiterate: The Marrone Law Firm did not represent her when she prepared her Estate Planning documents.

Wishing you the best of luck as you work through this process.

I have the signed contract stating it was supposed to be split between buyer and seller from my realtor, but Jen failed to record this in our closing documents. I contacted this firm regarding disability discrimination and retaliation after being terminated during the interactive process. I provided extensive documentation at intake, including HR emails, doctor’s notes, evidence of a failed interactive process, and a written timeline.

I want to clarify that I did not interact with the other partners. This review reflects only my experience with Mr. Gattuso.

While the paralegal I spoke with, Lindsay, was professional and compassionate, Mr. Gattuso did not appear to review the intake materials and dismissed my case using generic statements such as:

“Well, you can’t perform the essential functions of the job.”

“Well, it took them nine months to fire you.”

“We usually take stronger cases than this.”

“I have been to a hospital recently. It is a lot of movement, very fast-paced…”

These comments were made without addressing documented reassignment obligations, retaliation timing, or the employer’s months-long failure to engage in the interactive process — all of which were included in my intake materials. He also reduced my role as a Registered Nurse to a surface-level stereotype without reviewing the actual job descriptions or alternative RN-adjacent positions involved.

I was seeking contingency representation and a substantive legal evaluation. Instead, I received superficial conclusions disconnected from the evidence provided.

I would recommend this firm only with caution for ADA-related matters as, judging from other reviews, there does appear to be substance to the other partners.

If the rating was based on Lindsay alone, that would have easily been a 5-stars without question. She was punctual, compassionate and professional throughout. Because the rating should consider the experience as a whole, I cannot in good conscience provide that level of credit where it is not due.

After searching for an attorney outside my demographic area, I was pleased with my choice of Ciotoli and Gattuso. Stephen and Lindsay were very helpful with my case keeping me informed with next steps, etc.
They both were very responsive to emails and guiding me every step of the way.
Highly recommended!
Steve and Lindsay were really great to work with. They were supportive and very responsive and helped me resolve an employment issue.

I felt like I had a worthy employer related discrimination case and when I talked with Attorney Ryan Files, he agreed to help me. First let me say with a last name like "Files" that should let you know off top, he gets right down to business. When it comes to litigation and putting an opposing party on notice, Ryan Files is the "Big Gun". He was totally professional, but more importantly he is very experienced and knowledgeable, I felt like I could trust him, and get out of the way and let him do what he does. He made sure I was updated about everything in a timely manner and he made it inclusive so that I felt like I was in the room with him. I never had to call him or email him asking about my case because he handled my case in a prioritized manner, and it was settled very quickly. It's something about the way he applies the law, that made the opposing party bow down and bow out. We had an initial phone consultation and from there all communication and negotiations were done exclusively via email. I was very happy with the outcome, the kind of outcome that for me and my family was life changing. I highly recommend Attorney Ryan Files, because when he "Files" a case backed by Gattuso & Ciotoli they play to win win win!!!!!

Went to the law firm to try and set up a consultation. The administrative assistant asked me when I fill out the consultation form and to remind her of my case. She then said oh yeah the lawyers told me not to contact you because we couldn't help you with your case. 🤔 How am I supposed to know that if you never got back to me??? I'm not upset that you couldn't help me, I have had multiple other law firms tell me it's not in there area of expertise or something they deal with but they have told me that. I am just posting this so that others know and understand that if they fill out the consultation form or leave a message and don't here back it's probably because you are too busy to give a curtosy call and let them know that you can not assist them with their situation.

I was finally able to get a hold of him after months, thanks to the lovely paralegal Lindsay. Mr. Davies was very personable and helpful as he responded to our questions.

My husband and I used Fred Davies to prepare our Wills, Trust, Health Care Proxies and Powers of Attorney.  He and his staff are informative, professional and friendly, and we felt completely at ease with taking this step to protect our assets.  I highly recommend the Davies Law Firm.

Fred Davies and his team are friendly, attentive, and go out of their way to help their clients make the best decisions possible.  Their guidance and explanations on setting up a revocable trust were very much appreciated.

This guy is a grumpy old man and he will make you look bad if he represents you

Used William Balduf, Esq. for my commercial and personal real estate matters.  Glad I did.  He pointed out and insisted on corrections and details the other lawyers and title attorneys and company did not. Call Willliam Balduf if you are buying or selling your home.  It is money well spent.

When you get charged with a criminal offense it is hard to not panic and be sure you are having your interests represented.  Mr. Balduf handled a very serious matter for me and the outcome was better than I expected and the best part was it did not cost me a fortune.  Don't let the fancy ads fool you.

Typical greedy law firm only looking for easy money. My case is complicated, but the evidence of malpractice is overwhelming. In the past four months, I've been diagnosed with cancer and a very serious cardiac issue, and I have been complaining of symptoms related to both diagnoses for years now. I have a  LOT of medical documentation to prove that my doctors had made little or no effort to investigate the causes of my health issues. The intake rep stated that they would likely wait to proceed or make a final decision on the ability to properly assess my need for legal representation, and that someone would give me a return phone call. That never happened.  Apparently I wasn't worth a phone call from Michael Porter until I posted my review on Google, expressing my dissatisfaction with the lack of effort on Porter's handling of my inquiry.  To me, that is unacceptable and impersonal. I deserved better.

Porter law group, just like most of the law groups that say they set president, are frauds, false advertising. They fashion themselves as landmark case winners who set president. Yet, these so called landmark case winners only want to come on board once people pass preemptive challenges in the courts, and a judge has already determined a client's case has merit--and that's just to see what they can get out of the deal. They are, therefore, false advertising, because a landmark case is one where president is set, not where the president is already in place. Thanks for lying to the people. I will now be filing my own tort action, which only costs 210 dollars to submit.

My family and I are so grateful to have found Porter Law Group.
Aaron was exceptional and kept us informed throughout the entire process. Everyone I spoke with at the office was kind, courteous and caring.
We are very happy with the outcome of our case. The law firm was very accessible and met all my family's needs and demands.
I highly recommend this law firm because they will fight for you.
Claudia from West Seneca, NY

My wife Joanne was diagnosed in 2020 with cancer. It started a two year battle that sadly she lost in 2022. During her illness she contacted Porter Law Group to see if they could help her family with their future. Our son just married in 2019 and were expecting their first child in 2022. Joanne passed away before our grand daughter was born. Porter Law had been fighting for us all the while. Even though Joanne is gone, she is still taking care of her family with the help of Mike Porter and his team.

The Conyers Family

Our mother felt a lump in her breast. She went to have it checked and was told that it was benign. Months later, she went back to find out that it was a very aggressive form of breast cancer.  One night, I came across the commercial for Porter Law Group. The commercial asked the question my siblings, mother, and I had  “Did they catch her cancer in time”? My mother and I called Porter Law Group and was introduced to Mary Langan. I remember our first conversation after she reviewed my mothers documents and how she will take on my mothers case. Mary fought for our mom even when our mom couldn't do it herself. Mary was also very caring, understanding, and patient with myself and my siblings as we had no clue on how the law works, especially in terms of malpractice.  My mother passed away after having to fight this terrible disease. This still didn't stop Mary from fighting. With such dedication and knowledge and fierce determination, Mary and we came to a settlement. We are satisfied with how our mother was represented. Most importantly how Mary made sure our mother was given the most dignified amount, although money is not worth the life lost. When knowing something is not right and your loved one passes on, it's hard to grieve and try to move forward. However, Porter Law Group and Mary gave us the chance to grieve and heal without the expenses left behind. We will forever be grateful for them. Whenever I hear of someone going through similar situations, I always refer to Porter Law and Mary. They will fight for you even when you feel you can't.
The law firm of Porter&Porter were wonderful. They helped us through a difficult time and we were totally satisfied and would highly recommend them to anyone💜

Porter Law Group took my case when no one else would. Everyone was so nice and you can tell they care. From day one they always kept me updated on my case kept me going even when I was stating lose hope and give up. In the end it was definitely a life changing win ! Thank you all so much for your hard work and dedication.
The whole crew here are great.  Very on top of everything and great to deal with.  They have kept us informed every step of the way.
I very happy with the results in my case . I am live in Queen Ny . I will recommend  to any one they did a great job for my .

Excellent law firm, great results, knowledgeable and helpful caring law firm

We are so grateful to have found Porter Law Group to help us with our medical lawsuit. They are by far the best lawyers in New York! When we were not able to come to them, they came to us. The team fought exceptionally hard for a fair and quick settlement. The process was easy and we highly recommend them. We are very happy with the outcome of our case and cannot say enough good things about this law group.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for the kind words, JoAnn!

reviews.text:
I can't say enough about how pleased we are with Porter Law Group's handling of our case.  After my son was injured due to negligence, we contacted Porter Law Group to represent us in getting reimbursement with his medical bills and for possible future complications.  Our Attorney, Aaron Ryder, Esq, was professional while also empathetic towards our son.  He walked us through every step in the process so we always knew where the case stood and made sure we were involved with every decision throughout.

We thought this case could drag on for years but Aaron stayed on top of things and brought it to full resolution much sooner than we anticipated.  We ended up settling for much more than we thought we would get and now my son has money set aside in case he should need further surgery down the road.

We would definitely recommend Porter Law Group to anyone that needs legal representation.

My wife & I hired Porter Law Group after a diagnosis of breast cancer. She went every year for exams. We were devastated. Mike & Mary helped us tremendously during the process. I had three daughters and was lost. My wife passed 10 months after diagnosis and they fought for her, myself, and my 3 daughters. Nobody can bring back my wife or my daughter’s mother, but we can rest now that justice was served so hopefully someone else with cancer does not go misdiagnosed.

From the first meeting we were treated with respect and kindness by a team  of incredible people. My husband passed away from a very short yet painful cancer of liver that was undiagnosed and should’ve been treated. They made a very stressful time seem somewhat smooth  I was very happy with the outcome — albeit bitter sweet as my husband should have never gone through what he did  Thank you all.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your kind words. We are truly sorry for your loss and appreciate the trust you placed in us during such a difficult time. If you ever need anything, we’re here for you.

reviews.text:
You will be so happy with your decision to call Porter Law!

My dad passed away in November 2019 after being diagnosed with cancer in late June. Long story short, my dad's cancer could have been prevented. If my dad had been tested sooner by the people he trusted, his doctors, he may be here with us today.

I saw Mike's commercial and contacted his office. Mike responded quickly and believed in me and my family's story. With the help of his entire team, my mom and sister are able to worry much less about the financial burden of losing my dad - he really did everything! I wish my dad was going to walk me down the aisle when I get married in May. I miss skiing with him in the winter and hearing his funny jokes and great humor. I miss going to Arad Evans together after work. I miss him so much. Porter Law is the way to go! If you want someone who you can trust, who treats you like family, then don't wait. Call Porter.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your heartfelt words, Emily. We are so sorry for your loss and truly appreciate the trust you placed in us during such a difficult time. It means a lot to know that we could help bring some relief to your family.

reviews.text: Working with Eric and his firm was fantastic. Very knowledgeable and also very personable! Highly recommend!
